channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: ‘We are going make a tonne of money’: US Senator Graham on US war on Iran
item.description: The veteran GOP senator appears to suggest that the war on Iran was launched to gain control over its oil supplies.

item.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 10 of US-Israel attacks?
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has been selected as Iran’s new supreme leader. Attacks continue and oil prices have spiked.

item.title: Crowd chants ‘save our girls’ at bus carrying Iran women’s team
item.description: Crowds chased a bus carrying Iran’s women’s football team in Australia, chanting 'save our girls'.

item.title: Carney confirms: When Washington whistles, Ottawa salutes
item.description: The Canadian prime minister was quick to jump on the Iran war bandwagon, seemingly forgetting his own speech at Davos.

item.title: UAE releases video of Iranian drone interception
item.description: The UAE’s Ministry of Defence released video it says shows Iranian drones being intercepted and destroyed.

item.title: Body recovered from rubble in Tehran
item.description: Video released by the Iranian Red Crescent shows a body pulled from the rubble of a destroyed building in Tehran.

item.title: What asymmetric warfare may tell us about Iran’s fighting chances
item.description: In war, a weaker opponent will often resort to asymmetric warfare, a tactic Iran is likely to exploit against the US.

item.title: At least 42 people killed in days of floods across Kenya
item.description: Authorities say 26 people died in capital Nairobi as floodwaters submerged homes and carried away vehicles.

item.title: IRGC warns of energy war after US-Israeli strikes on Iranian assets
item.description: The spokesman for the IRGC, says the US has opened a new chapter in the war by bombing Iran’s energy infrastructure.

item.title: Bangladesh shuts universities, limits fuel sale as Iran war causes shortage
item.description: Bangladesh relies on imports for 95 percent of energy needs which have been disrupted after conflict in the Middle East.
